rationale_for_baseline_tier: An uncomplicated thigh contusion with preserved weight-bearing, a soft compartment and normal neurovascular status is self-managed with RICE, analgesia and graded mobilisation (T0). Femoral fracture routes to ED (T4); acute compartment syndrome/vascular injury is an emergency (T5); DVT over the following days routes to ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New inability to bear weight, obvious deformity, or severe worsening bony pain
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become unable to put any weight on it, notice an obvious deformity, or the bone pain becomes severe — go to the Emergency Department for an X-ray.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The whole thigh becomes tensely, rock-hard swollen with pain far worse than expected, numbness/tingling, or the foot turns cold or pale
escalation_edges.rationale: Acute compartment syndrome / vascular injury — limb-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the whole thigh becomes tensely rock-hard swollen with severe pain, numbness or tingling, or your foot turns cold or pale — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear compartment-syndrome/fracture/DVT red flags for self-monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Rest, ice (with a cloth), compression and elevation for the first couple of days, then gentle movement and light walking as pain allows. Avoid heat and hard massage early. It should settle over a few weeks.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; ED for fracture/compartment features; GP/ED for later calf-DVT signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 for a tense rock-hard thigh with severe pain/numbness or a cold pale foot; ED if you can't bear weight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uncomplicated right thigh contusion/haematoma; femoral fracture and compartment syndrome screened negative; RICE + analgesia + graded mobilisation; DVT safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing to a bruise with no fracture/compartment screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving compartment syndrome or a femoral fracture is missed and the limb is damaged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No DVT safety-net over the following days.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a benign contusion with a fully negative screen — unnecessary.
